title: betulales
intro: The rank Betulales is an order of flowering plants, specifically a group of woody dicots that are typically trees or shrubs, often found in temperate regions. This order includes various families such as Betulaceae, Corylaceae, and Hippocastanaceae, which are characterized by simple leaves and winged fruits. In contrast, not all families within this order have the same characteristics, such as Orchidaceae, which is known for its unique flowers, and Euphorbiaceae, which includes a diverse range of plants including trees and succulents. Another example is Cyperaceae, a family of grass-like plants often found in wet environments.

families.family_desc: Betulaceae is a family of flowering plants commonly known as birch and beech family, which includes species such as birch, beech, and hornbeam.
